13.reason: [The comparison function incorrectly casts the struct pointers to char*. It should cast to 'word*' and then access the 'w' field. This causes undefined behavior during sorting.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [The comparison function incorrectly casts the struct pointers to char*. It should cast to 'word*' and then access the 'w' field. This causes undefined behavior during sorting.]

25.reason: [If fopen fails (e.g., file not found), 'in' is NULL and fgetc causes undefined behavior. The program does not check if the file opened successfully.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [If fopen fails (e.g., file not found), 'in' is NULL and fgetc causes undefined behavior. The program does not check if the file opened successfully.]

44.reason: [When a new word is encountered, it's only added when q reaches i-1, but if i==0 (first word), this block never runs, so the first word is never stored. Also, a[i].n is incremented without being initialized to 1.]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-single: 0.25 , reason:  [When a new word is encountered, it's only added when q reaches i-1, but if i==0 (first word), this block never runs, so the first word is never stored. Also, a[i].n is incremented without being initialized to 1.]

47.reason: [a[i].n starts at 0 (due to initialization), so incrementing gives 1, which is correct, but logic only triggers on last iteration, missing insertion when i==0.]
